skill.name: hubspot-automation
skill.description: Automate HubSpot CRM contacts, companies, deals, tickets, and properties through Rube MCP and Composio's HubSpot toolkit.
skill.summary: Guide AI agents through common HubSpot CRM workflows using Rube MCP tools.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged many inline backtick references as Ruby or shell execution, but SKILL.md is documentation and contains no executable Ruby or shell code. The only confirmed risk factor is a documented external Rube MCP URL used for HubSpot OAuth workflows, so publication is reasonable with normal third-party integration disclosure.

low_findings.title: False positive: inline MCP tool references flagged as shell execution
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported many Ruby or shell backtick findings. The inspected lines use Markdown inline code for Rube and HubSpot MCP tool names, parameters, and response fields, not executable commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: SKILL.md is plain Markdown guidance and the backticks wrap tool slugs or parameter names. I found no Ruby syntax, shell command invocation, or instruction to execute local commands.

low_findings.title: False positive: weak cryptography alert on description text
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged a weak cryptographic algorithm on the frontmatter description line. That line only describes HubSpot CRM automation and does not contain cryptographic code or algorithm selection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is descriptive metadata for the skill. No evidence of hashing, encryption, signing, or cryptographic library usage appears in the file.

low_findings.title: False positive: CRM search guidance flagged as reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged system reconnaissance on lines that recommend HubSpot searches before creating records. These are CRM duplicate-prevention steps, not local host or network reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines discuss HubSpot contact and company lookup to prevent duplicate CRM records. I found no evidence of filesystem, process, host, account, or network enumeration.

low_findings.title: External MCP endpoint requires user trust
low_findings.description: The setup instructions direct users to add the Rube MCP endpoint for HubSpot operations. This is expected for the integration, but it sends CRM workflow actions through a third-party MCP service.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The external URL is clearly present and is semantically tied to the required Rube MCP setup. It appears legitimate for the stated purpose, but users should still review the third-party service before connecting HubSpot.

content.value_statement: HubSpot CRM work often requires exact tool names, property names, pagination, and batch limits. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structured workflows for using HubSpot through Rube MCP.
actual_capabilities: Guides contact creation, contact search, and duplicate prevention workflows.
Documents company creation, company search, and company update tool sequences.
Explains deal and pipeline lookup using HubSpot pipeline, stage, and owner tools.
Supports ticket search workflows with filters, properties, sorting, and pagination.
Covers custom property discovery, creation, batch creation, and update patterns.
Highlights HubSpot batch limits, internal property names, and cursor pagination.
limitations: Requires a configured Rube MCP server and an active HubSpot connection.
Does not include executable scripts, local automation code, or direct API clients.
Cannot bypass HubSpot permissions, OAuth requirements, or account-level limits.
Users must validate property names and enum values against their own HubSpot portal.

use_cases.title: Prepare CRM Import Workflows
use_cases.description: Create a repeatable contact or company import plan with duplicate checks and 100-record batch chunks.

use_cases.title: Audit Sales Pipeline Data
use_cases.description: Find deal records by pipeline, stage, owner, close date, and amount fields using the correct HubSpot search patterns.

use_cases.title: Extend HubSpot Object Schemas
use_cases.description: Plan custom property creation with internal names, groups, field types, and enumeration options.

prompt_templates.title: Check My HubSpot Connection
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the HubSpot automation skill to verify my Rube MCP HubSpot connection. Start by finding the current HubSpot tool schemas, then confirm account access.

prompt_templates.title: Create Contacts Safely
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the HubSpot automation skill to create these contacts. Search for existing records first, map the required properties, and split work into valid batch sizes.

prompt_templates.title: Search Deals by Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the HubSpot automation skill to find deals in a named pipeline and stage. Resolve pipeline and stage IDs before searching deals.

prompt_templates.title: Design Custom Properties
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the HubSpot automation skill to design and create custom properties for a HubSpot object. Check existing properties, property groups, and enum options first.

output_examples.input: Create 250 company records from a prepared list.
output_examples.output: The assistant would search existing companies, divide the import into three batches, create valid company records, and return created IDs for follow-up.

output_examples.input: Find open support tickets updated this month.
output_examples.output: The assistant would inspect ticket properties, build the correct filter criteria, paginate through ticket results, and summarize matching records.

output_examples.input: Add a custom deal property for renewal risk.
output_examples.output: The assistant would check existing deal properties and groups, propose an internal property name, then create the field with approved options.

best_practices: Call RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS first so tool schemas match the current Rube MCP environment.
Search before creating contacts or companies to reduce duplicate CRM records.
Use internal HubSpot property names and validate enum values before writes.
anti_patterns: Do not assume HubSpot display labels are valid property names.
Do not send more than 100 records in one batch operation.
Do not continue write workflows after account or OAuth verification fails.

faq.question: Does this skill connect directly to HubSpot?
faq.answer: No. It provides instructions for using HubSpot tools exposed through Rube MCP.

faq.question: What setup is required?
faq.answer: Users need Rube MCP configured and an active HubSpot OAuth connection for the HubSpot toolkit.

faq.question: Can it create or update CRM records?
faq.answer: Yes, when the connected HubSpot account has permission and the user asks the AI agent to perform those actions.

faq.question: Does it prevent duplicate contacts?
faq.answer: It instructs the agent to search existing contacts and companies before creating new records.

faq.question: Can it discover custom property names?
faq.answer: Yes. It recommends using HubSpot property discovery tools before filtering, writing, or creating properties.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found documentation-only content and no malicious instructions, with a low risk from the required external MCP endpoint.
